stops.title: Your at-a-glance answer
stops.body: This banner answers "is anything wrong with any student's work right now?" Sage = all caught up. Amber = a few things worth a look. Wine = needs attention. The number on the right of the topbar shows the same count.

stops.title: Your students
stops.body: Click any name to load that student's profile. The dashed "Add" pill lets you onboard a new student. Pills colour in once a student has baseline samples.

stops.title: Score a new submission
stops.body: Paste a piece of student writing here and click Analyse. The result returns a percentage authenticity score, a tier breakdown, and a recommendation written in plain English. Click "Technical view" on any metric for the raw deviation.

stops.title: Build the voice profile
stops.body: Add baseline samples (essays, papers) to strengthen a student's voice profile. "Request proctored sitting via Bbook" emails the student a one-sitting magic link — proctored samples carry the highest trust weight.

stops.title: Outstanding proctored sittings
stops.body: When you request proctored sittings, they appear in this pill until the student completes them. Click it any time to see who's outstanding. The hero answer above mirrors this count.

stops.title: Is your voice growing?
stops.body: This banner answers "is my voice growing or holding steady?" Sage = strengthening or steady. Amber = settling, worth keeping an eye on. Wine = a recent piece reads a little differently — an invitation, never a verdict.

stops.title: Your seven dimensions
stops.body: Each axis is a blend of how you write — Cadence, Diction, Texture, Register, Restraint, Architecture, Resonance. The shape is yours. None of these axes corresponds to a single underlying number, so it cannot be reverse-engineered.

stops.title: Voice across the term
stops.body: Each point is one piece of work. The value is a 0–100 fidelity score — higher means the system recognises this piece as yours. The arc shows whether you are strengthening, holding steady, or moving.

stops.title: Voice Sampled · Established · Affirmed
stops.body: Three named milestones. Not deadlines, not grades — they describe what is already true about your record. The third is reached when a verified piece (proctored or instructor-confirmed) is on file.

stops.title: Add to your record
stops.body: Submit Work adds a piece to your profile. Each submission appears on the arc and contributes to the fingerprint. Nothing here is graded — this is your formation record.

stops.title: Is everything healthy?
stops.body: This banner answers "is the system healthy?" The status comes from the worst KPI — if any students are flagged for attention, the banner reflects that. Sage means quiet.

stops.title: Students · Baselines · Submissions · Attention
stops.body: Four numbers that summarise the institution: how many students are enrolled, how many baseline samples are on file, how many submissions have been scored, and how many students are currently in the attention queue.

stops.title: Every student at a glance
stops.body: The full institutional roster. Click any row to jump into the professor's view of that student. The colour bar at the right shows their voice-purity at a glance.

stops.title: What just happened
stops.body: Live feed of recent submissions across the institution — who submitted what, when, and the action the system recommended. A scrollable institutional log.

stops.title: Are any tenants in trouble?
stops.body: This banner answers "are any of my tenants in trouble?" A tenant counts as troubled when it has registered students but no baseline samples yet — onboarded but not yet operating.

stops.title: Across all tenants
stops.body: Total institutions, students, submissions, and audit entries — the operator's cross-tenant view. Each tenant's contribution is broken out in the cards below.

stops.title: Every tenant in one place
stops.body: Each card is one school. Click View → professor.html scoped to that tenant. Click View Stats for a quick per-tenant breakdown.

stops.title: Onboard a new tenant
stops.body: Register the next school. After registration, the school's professor signs in and starts building voice profiles. The system stays multi-tenant — students never cross schools.
